Anaïs Legand, from the WHO’s high threat pathogens team, said the revised death rate estimate is based on confirmed cases. “It’s huge. It means that up to five out of 10 people are likely to die,” Legand told reporters in Geneva.

articles.title: Friday briefing: ​What do the cuts in aid mean for the fight against Ebola in the DRC?
articles.description:
In today’s newsletter: As the virus spreads across borders, health workers warn that weakened global support is making a prolonged crisis more likely

Ebola is spreading rapidly in parts of east Africa. The deadly disease, which kills around half of those it infects, is suspected to have claimed the lives of at least 240 people since the outbreak began in Ituri province in the Democratic Republic of the Congo earlier this month.
Public health officials are scrambling to contain the virus in one of the toughest environments: Ituri province, the centre of the crisis, is a mining hub where thousands of people work in close proximity every day, and a conflict zone, with ongoing fighting between rebel groups. Medical facilities are modest, while waves of displaced people are being forced into overcrowded camps to escape fighting, making it even harder to control transmission. The virus has already spread to other regions in eastern DRC and the Ugandan capital Kampala.

The head of the World Health Organization has called for an immediate ceasefire in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo to help tackle the Ebola outbreak there, as Uganda closed its border with its neighbour in an effort to stop the spread.
Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus posted on social media that the region was in the midst of a “catastrophic collision of disease and conflict with the Ebola outbreak in Ituri province outpacing the response”. Tedros said on Monday that he would travel to the DRC this week.

articles.title: The deadly plane attack at the centre of Castro's indictment

articles.title: Countless endangered species are making a comeback due to global conservation efforts

articles.title: The death of a relentless mother reopens the wound of Venezuela’s political repression

articles.title: Rosa Pachurí Paraba: How Monkox indigenous women are leading Bolivia’s response to wildfires
articles.description: Bolivia’s 2024 wildfires devastated Santa Cruz, but Indigenous Chiquitano and Monkox communities led recovery through collective organization, ancestral knowledge and fire management strategies, and revived food systems amid delayed state response.
articles.titleJa: ロサ・パチュリ・パラバ：モンコックス先住民女性がボリビアの山火事対応を主導する方法

articles.title: Attacks on trans identities become a strategic political tool in Brazil
articles.description: In the last decade in Brazil, transphobia has become one of the main tools for disseminating misinformation. In many cases, it is spread by elected politicians with millions of followers
articles.titleJa: ブラジルでトランスジェンダーのアイデンティティへの攻撃が戦略的政治ツールとなる

articles.title: Dominican Republic: Is protecting your environment a way to love your country?

articles.title: Across Latin America, May Day protests highlight workers’ struggles and solidarity

articles.title: Who is defending Cuba’s threatened species?

articles.titleJa: キューバの絶滅危惧種を守っているのは誰か？

articles.title: The human cost of the data center push in Latin America

articles.title: Rescuers race to free seven people trapped in flooded Laos cave

articles.title: BTS win big as Black Eyed Peas reunite at American Music Awards
articles.description: Other winners included Golden, from the film K-Pop Demon Hunters, Sabrina Carpenter and Katseye.

articles.title: New Zealand launches safeguard investigation on certain aluminium extrusions
articles.description: On 27 May 2026, New Zealand notified the WTO’s Committee on Safeguards of the initiation on 28 May 2026 of a safeguard investigation on imports of certain aluminium extrusions.
articles.titleJa: ニュージーランド、特定アルミニウム押出材に対するセーフガード調査を開始

articles.title: Shipping industry cites mounting cost, capacity pressures amid global trade disruptions
articles.description: Senior executives from leading global shipping companies and industry groups underlined growing operational and economic challenges to goods trade during a 28 May meeting with WTO Director-General Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala. They noted that, while global supply chains have demonstrated resilience, efforts to identify and rely on alternative maritime routes amid disruptions – particularly in the Gulf region and other chokepoints – are driving up costs for shippers and ultimately consumers.
articles.titleJa: 海運業界、世界貿易の混乱の中で増大するコストと輸送能力の圧力を指摘
articles.summary: 世界の海運業界幹部がWTO事務局長と会談し、湾岸地域などの海上輸送路の混乱により、輸送コストが増加し消費者物価に影響を与えていると述べた。

articles.title: DDG Nordquist calls for urgent action on fisheries subsidies at Monaco Blue Initiative
articles.description: Deputy Director-General DJ Nordquist called for urgent action by WTO members to complete the acceptance and implementation of the Agreement on Fisheries Subsidies and to advance negotiations on additional disciplines targeting overcapacity and overfishing. She delivered her remarks on 27 May at the Monaco Blue Initiative in Monaco. DDG Nordquist stressed that harmful fisheries subsidies continue to undermine ocean sustainability, economic resilience, and coastal livelihoods, while emphasizing that sustainable fisheries are essential for jobs, food security, and long-term prosperity. Her full remarks are below.
articles.titleJa: ノードキスト事務次長、モナコ海洋イニシアティブで漁業補助金に関する緊急行動を要請
articles.summary: ノードキストWTO事務次長は、漁業補助金協定の採択・実施を急ぐよう求め、有害な補助金が海洋の持続可能性や経済的安定を損なうと強調した。

articles.title: Trade Policy Review: Japan
articles.description: The sixteenth review of the trade policies and practices of Japan takes place on 27 and 29 May 2026. The basis for the review is a report by the WTO Secretariat and a report by the Government of Japan.
articles.titleJa: 貿易政策検討：日本

articles.title: Members consider Chinese request for dispute panel on solar, IT goods measures in India
articles.description: At a meeting of the Dispute Settlement Body (DSB) on 22 May, members considered a request from China for the establishment of a dispute panel to review measures in India affecting imports of solar cells, solar modules and information technology goods. The new DSB Chair, Ambassador Guilherme de Aguiar Patriota (Brazil), also made a statement on advancing the WTO's dispute settlement reform talks.
articles.titleJa: 中国、インドにおける太陽光発電・IT製品措置に関する紛争処理小委員会設置を要請

articles.title: Members address current trade tensions, transparency at Goods Council meeting
articles.description: Members emphasized the growing strain on the rules-based trading system amid escalating trade tensions and compliance challenges at a meeting of the WTO’s Goods Council on 20-21 May chaired by its recently elected Chairperson, Ambassador Erwin Bollinger (Switzerland). Delegations in attendance addressed 38 specific trade concerns raised by members, of which nine were raised for the first time.
articles.titleJa: 物品理事会会合で貿易摩擦と透明性について議論

articles.title: Russian Federation notifies launch of safeguard investigation on motor car pneumatic tyres

articles.titleJa: ロシア連邦、自動車用空気入りタイヤに関するセーフガード調査開始を通報

articles.title: Members advance transparency work in rules of origin, explore trade facilitation links
articles.description: At a meeting of the Committee on Rules of Origin (CRO) on 11-12 May, WTO members advanced work on transparency and notification practices, discussed the future direction of work on preferential rules of origin for least-developed countries (LDCs), and participated in an information session on the links between trade facilitation and rules of origin. The meeting was chaired by Ms Carol Tsang of Hong Kong, China.
articles.titleJa: メンバー、原産地規則の透明性向上作業を進め、貿易円滑化との関連を探る

articles.title: Sweden contributes SEK 2 million to WTO capacity-building activities
articles.description: Sweden has given SEK 2 million (just under CHF 170,000) to the WTO's Global Trust Fund for 2026 to finance technical assistance and training programmes for government officials from developing economies and least-developed countries (LDCs). The contribution was confirmed at a signing ceremony on 8 May at the WTO attended by Ambassador Nina Tornberg of Sweden and the WTO's Director of the Finance and Corporate Services Division, Andrew Rizk.
articles.titleJa: スウェーデン、WTO能力構築活動に200万スウェーデン・クローナを拠出
articles.summary: スウェーデンが2026年分のWTO世界信託基金に200万スウェーデン・クローナを拠出し、途上国の政府職員向け技術支援・研修プログラムを支援する。

articles.title: General Council chair outlines next steps to build on momentum from MC14 negotiations
articles.description: At the first meeting of the General Council since the 14th Ministerial Conference (MC14), the newly elected Chairperson, Ambassador Clare Kelly (New Zealand), reported on her consultations with members on how to continue discussions in Geneva to build upon the high level of convergence on several areas of work at MC14 held in Yaoundé, Cameroon. Members also took note of Türkiye's announcement that it was joining the growing convergence to extend the e-commerce moratorium, with many welcoming the additional progress in narrowing gaps in the discussions.
articles.titleJa: 一般理事会議長、MC14交渉の勢いを活かすための次なるステップを説明
articles.summary: WTO一般理事会で、MC14後の交渉進展に向けた協議結果が報告され、特に電子商取引モラトリアム延長に関するトルコの参加が注目された。
